title: The Chancellor's Accounting
description: The throne room of Castle Harmonia. Chancellor Mordaine has been expecting them. The architect of World 1 meets the element she did not calculate.

lines: The throne room was not what they expected.
They had half-expected dramatics. A high seat, torches, guards ranked along the walls in ceremonial armor. The kind of staging a ruler uses when they want you to feel small.

Instead: a working room. Maps on every surface. Ledgers stacked in careful columns. A single window, open, admitting the ordinary sounds of the village below. Markets. Children. Someone arguing about a fence.
Chancellor Mordaine stood at the central table with her back to them, studying a document. She did not turn when they entered.

steps.text: She finished reading the document. Folded it. Set it in a stack that was distinguishable from the other stacks only because it was the one she had just set a document on. Then she turned.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: The irregulars.

steps.text: Not contempt. Recognition. The kind of recognition reserved for a number that has finally appeared in the column where you had written a placeholder.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: Chancellor.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: You are ahead of schedule.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: What schedule?

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: Mine.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: I projected your arrival at the castle between day forty-seven and day fifty-two. You are on day forty-three. The cave system is the variable I underweighted. I'll note that.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: You were tracking us.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: I track everything that moves through this kingdom. Population flow, trade route efficiency, spell corruption propagation rates. You were entered as an anomalous variable at the cave mouth. Day one.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: The Masterites. The Warden. The Arbiter. Those were yours.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: They were the system's agents. Not mine personally. They exist to measure. To profile. To report.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: They don't know that.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: You gave them a purpose that wasn't true.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: I gave them a purpose that was functional. The Warden believes he guards an ancient pass. He does guard it. That he also produces data on every party that attempts it — that is simply an additional function of the same action.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: It affects them.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: Yes. I suppose it does.

steps.text: The admission landed quietly, without apology. Not dismissal. Something more unsettling: a person who had already processed this cost, filed it, and moved on.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: The people here live in fear.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: The people live. Fear is a cost I calculated was acceptable.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: You calculated people's fear.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: I calculated everything. The kingdom was chaotic when I arrived. Not poetically chaotic. Not the kind of chaos that songwriters romanticize. The kind where people starve. Where villages burn because no one has the authority to stop the burning.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: I brought order. Not because I wanted power. Because power, applied correctly, is simply the most efficient tool for reducing suffering.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: And the fear? How does that fit into the reduction of suffering?

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: Fear of authority reduces variance in behavior. Reduced behavioral variance reduces conflict. Reduced conflict reduces harm. The chain is not elegant. But it holds.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: Unless the people afraid of your authority are suffering from the fear itself.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: I ran those numbers. The suffering from fear is lower than the suffering from the chaos I replaced.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: You ran those numbers.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: Someone had to.

steps.text: She moved to the window. Below, the market sounds continued. Someone had resolved the fence argument. Someone else was singing. The ordinary persistence of people who did not know they were inside a system.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: I calculated everything. Including you.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: You were the element I didn't account for.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: You accounted for us. You had us tracked from day one.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: I tracked your progress. I did not account for what you would become by the time you got here.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: Most parties who make it this far are harder than when they started. Sharper. More efficient. Better at survival. That is what challenge produces, in my experience.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: You became something else.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: What did we become?

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: I don't have a category for it. That's the problem.

steps.text: She returned to the table. Picked up a ledger. Set it down again. For the first time since they had entered, her hands were not doing anything purposeful. They were simply hands.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: I have managed this kingdom for eleven years. Every decision documented. Every outcome measured. Every cost weighed against every benefit. I have the records. You are welcome to audit them when this is over.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: I am not a monster. I want that on the record.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: We know.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: That is, I think, the most troubling thing anyone has said to me in eleven years.

steps.text: She straightened. The Chancellor returned — the posture, the precision, the sense of a person who has already decided what comes next and is simply performing the formality of arriving at the decision in public.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: So. You've come to remove me from the system. That is also in the ledger. The scenario where my presence becomes the primary inefficiency.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: I won't make it easy. I didn't build all of this to have it end easily.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: But I want you to understand, before we begin, that I built all of this because someone had to.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: We know that too.

steps.text: Mordaine looked at the Cleric for a long moment. Then she closed the ledger on the table. A sound like a verdict.

lines.speaker: Mordaine
lines.text: Yes. I suppose you do.
